description_long: This object is an irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly muted pinkish hue, interspersed with subtle patches of white and hints of gray. Its surface is rough and uneven, featuring natural indentations, small protrusions, and a granular texture typical of weathered rock. The stone appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. One side displays a slightly flatter area, while the rest of the stone is more rounded and lumpy, suggesting it may have been naturally tumbled or broken from a larger formation. The coloration and texture indicate it could be a type of sandstone or similar sedimentary rock, commonly found in urban landscaping or as a barrier in parks. There are no visible carvings, inscriptions, or signs of human alteration, giving it a raw, natural appearance.
description: A rough, pinkish stone with irregular shape, granular texture, and subtle white and gray patches. Its surface is uneven and natural, typical of weathered rock.
description_view_0: This view shows the stone's broadest, flattest side, highlighting its pinkish color and a few white patches on the upper surface.
description_view_1: From this angle, the stone appears more upright and reveals a rounded end with a visible white spot and some subtle grayish areas.
description_view_2: This side profile emphasizes the stone's elongated shape and layered texture, with a prominent white patch near the top edge.
description_view_3: This view displays the opposite end, showing a more angular surface with a white patch and a slightly rougher, less uniform texture.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a low barrier along paths
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling with irregular white patches and faint layering, no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and granular with minor surface pitting, indicating moderate natural weathering and exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones in landscaped areas, providing a subtle contrast to concrete or manicured lawns.
